details.text:
كٓهيعٓصٓ

- These are from the Huroof Al-Muqatta’aat (الحُرُوفُ المُقَطَّعات), letters which are cut apart and separated from each other, with which some of the Suwar of the Qur’an begin with. The majority view is that none but Allah knows their meaning although some of the Sahabah and Scholars have provided some meanings for these letters. Fakhrudin Ar-Razi indicated that Allah begins with these in order to present something that we don’t understand in order to put our intellects in their place, so that we approach the Qur’an with humility and the doors of knowledge, understanding and guidance will be opened for us.
Derivative: سَوِيًّا can be translated as consecutive or complete, however, سَوِيًّا can also mean healthy.

Derivative: A place where worship is undertaken. The origin of ٱلْمِحْرَابِ is حَرب which means war. It can be understood that this is the place that one makes war against Shaytan and battle against your Nafs. ٱلْمِحْرَابِ also can mean the place where you prepare and keep the war arsenal. ٱلْمِحْرَابِ can also refer to the first place of a house and a place where people gather to make plans.
Derivative: أَوْحَىٰٓ here means that he gave indications, signals and pointers. وَحِي means إِشَارَة: To point to and indicate to by clues and signalling.
Derivative: صَبِيًّا is used to refer to a child but is also used for an adult who acts like a child.
Derivative: ٱلْحُكْمَ refers to maturity and firmness in decision.

Derivative: حَنَان refers to missing something and being unable to stay away from it for too long. This is like a mother with her child or in the case of Yahya (as) refers to His relationship with Allah and undertaking Dhikr.
Derivative: Very watchful, careful and protective (of his character).
- in addition to meaning “peace being upon Him” it also carries the meaning of “being honoured”.

Derivative: نَبَذَ means to throw away something without value. اِنْتَبَذَ is when someone does not value themselves or does not regard themselves worthy (in humility and humbleness) and cast themselves out from other peoples’ company.
- The implication of مَكَانًا شَرْقِيًّا (an Eastern place or location) is east of Masjid Al-Aqsa, a place that was more isolated.

Derivative: سَوِيًّا
- سَوِيًّا: upright, healthy in character – appearing to be a righteous man.
Derivative: تَمَثَّلَ means he represented himself or to come in the form of.
Derivative: Consciousness of Allah.

- Meaning can be: If you have any consciousness of Allah “then you would not do anything improper” in the case where the latter part is omitted but can be understood. Or it could also mean: “Even if you are righteous and have consciousness of Allah” (I still seek the refuge of Allah from you).
Derivative: زَكِيّ means intelligent and also pure of character.
- Grammar points: عَبْدَهُۥ is Mansub (مَنْصوب) here because it is the Maf’ul Bihi (مَفْعول بِهِ) of ذِكْرُ. And زَكَرِيَّا is the ‘Atf ul-Bayan (عَطْف البَيان) of عَبْدَهُۥ.
-This is an adjective on top of an adjective. I wish that I was completely and utterly forgotten about where nobody would even remember who I was i.e., erased from memory.

- This phrase is used not to literally mean that I wish that I was dead but rather it implies the meaning that I wish that I didn’t have to take on this responsibility as I am not sure that I am capable of bearing it.
Derivative: سَرِيٌّ is a small stream of water.
Derivative: The صَوْم (fast) related to speaking where you would declare the number of days in which you would not speak to anybody.
- It literally means “And cool the eye” with the meaning that can include to: Relax, calm down and stop crying.
Derivative: فَرِيٌّ refers to someone who has done something unexpected and a very big crime – a terrible horrible thing that is no small matter.
- Literally means “O sister of Harun”. It can be referring to the actual sibling of Maryam called Harun just as it could be a reference to the descendants or line of Harun (as).
Derivative: Means very quiet, secretive and hidden. It means so quiet and secretive that people are unaware of its existence. A secret so well guarded that people are not even looking for it.
Derivative: نِدَآءً is a call that is loud as it needs to carry over a long distance. The نِدَآءً in contrast to the دُعاء (call) implies greater desperateness.

- This brings together two contrasting meanings. The first نِدَآءً of something loud and the second خَفِيًّا of something that is quiet and hidden. As such, in this most quiet manner, he made this call to His Rabb. Even though he is desperate, de doesn’t allow his voice to be raised.

Derivative: أَوْصَى differs in meaning from نَصِيحَة (Advice) and أَمْر (command). أَوْصَى relates to the strongest and most prioritised thing that is being asked to be undertake, not just out of obligation but also out of concern for the person being asked to undertake it.
- The connection between جَبَّارًا (a tyrant) and شَقِيًّا (unfortunate/wretched) in the case where being a tyrant leads to misfortune. You will have a life of شَقِيّ if you are جَبَّار.

- قَوْلَ ٱلْحَقِّ means: The word of truth with “I say” being implied but omitted because it is in حالَة النَّصْب with a Fat’hah قَوْلَ. Some Qira’aat have قَوْلُ ٱلْحَقّ in حالَةُ الرَّفْع with a Dammah قَوْلُ which means: It is the word of Al-Haqq (ٱلْحَقّ) meaning it is the word of The Truth Manifest. With both meanings it dispels all doubt regarding the truth concerning Isa (as).
Derivative: مَّشْهَدِ – The witnessing and the time to be witnessed.
- This is Usloob At-Ta’ajjub (أُسْلوب التَّعَجُّب) – A style of emphasis, exaggeration and showing great wonder. It means: “How well they will hear and how well they will hear”.
Derivative: شَيْب is used in Arabic to refer to old age which is indicated to by white hair.
Derivative: شَقِيٌّ means unfortunate and can also mean wretched which can be indicative of being disappointed.
Derivative: ٱشْتَعَلَ means to catch on fire. To turn into flame and be reduced to ash.
Derivative: وَهَنَ is a form of weakness when something hard becomes soft.

- اِشْتَعَلَ is used because when fire burns something and turns it to white ash, there is no undoing it and it is beyond repair. Similarly, when the hair has gone white, he is indicating that he has reached an age that there is no return from and my white hair is a deterioration that I can’t come back from.

- My bone has become weak مَنِّي “despite me”. He did not say العِظام (the bones i.e., the plural) but rather used the singular ٱلْعَظْمُ (the bone) as if he is saying all of them feel like one bone and that bone has become weak. When the bones become weak the whole thing becomes weak. The use of مِنِّى (literally: from me) indicates his separating himself from his bones as if they don’t belong to him anymore.

Derivative: صِدِّيق means “ever truthful” in the case where truthful means that every time Allah asked him to do something he fulfilled it immediately. Abu Bakr was also called صِدِّيق because confirmed the truth immediately and fulfilled its rights fully.

- There is a difference in Arabic between saying أَبِي (my father) and يَٰٓأَبَتِ in the case where يَٰٓأَبَتِ includes love, appreciation and respect in its meaning. The أَب part refers to the father and the ت refers to love, appreciation and respect expressed to the father.
Derivative: سَوِيّ is something that is full and healthy and has no gaps in it.
Derivative: Keep away or migrate away from me.
Derivative: For a long period of time.
- the اِسْم الفاعِل (noun of the doer) is used here instead of the فِعْل (verb) which can be seen to add extra emphasis, Like He is saying: are you really committed to turning away from my Gods?
Derivative: حَفِيٌّ is someone who asks and enquires about someone over and over again. Here is implies that Allah will check up upon him and take care of him.
Derivative: اِعْتِزال is to cut yourself off, withdraw and isolate.
Derivative: Hopefully indicating to not being sure about what lies in the future.

Derivative: عَاقِر in Arabic literally means to bite or to cut something (from its roots). It is also used for the last child someone has عَقْرٌ. عَاقِر can also mean an injury or a gash/cut. عَاقِر can also refer classically to a woman who has had some trauma or sickness and as a result she could not have children anymore.
Derivative: ٱلْمَوَٰلِىَ means my guardians, the plural of مَوْلَى, and can imply those people from my family that are close and who will inherit my legacy and responsibilities.
- A tongue that speaks the truth with a high tongue that doesn’t resort to low language.
Derivative: Someone made sincere and chosen.

- An Arabic principle states that if two words are separate from each other they can have the same meaning but if they are brought together, then they differ in meaning. Because رَسُول and نَبِيّ have been brought together it has led to discussion about the differences between the two and has resulted in differences of opinion regarding the distinction between the two.
Derivative: نَجِيًّا means to take into confidence and have a private secret meeting.
Derivative: We granted or we gave as a gift. For that reason Harun (as) has been referred to in classical scholarship as هِبَةُ الله – the gift of Allah which was given to Musa (as).
- The fulfiller of the promise, referring to the promise that he would comply with his father and fulfil the vision that he saw.
Derivative: The one whom Allah is pleased with.
Derivative: صِدِّيق The one who confirms the truth immediately.
Derivative: مَكَانًا literally means a place but figuratively can mean status.
Derivative: Means to waste which implies that it is something of value that hasn’t been taken advantage of.

Derivative: There is a difference between خَلَف (Khalaf) and خَلْفٌ (Khalf). When you leave behind good generations they are referred to as خَلَف whilst bad or disappointing generations that you leave behind are referred to as خَلْفٌ. Insight into the significance of changing Harakaat in the words of the Arabic language: The فَتْحَة (Fat’hah) on the middle radical is an indication of growth but when the Harakah is taken away from the word and replaced with a سُكون (Sukoon) it can indicate to the lack of growth and stagnancy. خَلَف is a generation that moved and was productive whilst خَلْفٌ is a generation that sat behind and did nothing.
Derivative: شَهَوات are the baser animal temptations (desires) of human beings.
Derivative: غَيًّا means that you have gone so far off track or astray, that you can’t find your way back.
- they will meet with misguidance means that they will meet the consequences of misguidance.
Derivative: رَضِي is the meaning someone who is content and does not want more. An alternative meaning that can be understood here is someone whom Allah will be pleased with.

- Firstly يَرِثُنِى literally means to inherit from me but this inheritance refers to knowledge and responsibilities as Prophets are not inherited from in terms of wealth and properties.
- وَيَرِثُ مِنْ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ Here the مِنْ is indicative of some of the family of Ya’qub and not all of them.
Derivative: مَأْتِيًّا Something that will be fulfilled and become reality without doubt.
Derivative: لَغْوًا refers to any type of useless, insulting, empty and pointless talk.
Derivative: سَمِيًّا – Something with the same qualities and equal.
- The addition of مَا implies that he believes his death is far away, so, it carries the meaning of: “When I die one day eventually”.
Derivative: جِثِيًّا means to be sat on their knees.
Derivative: نَزَعَ means to خَلْع (to yank and pull out) something from its origin with force.
Derivative: عِتِيًّا means intransigence and rebelliousness.
Derivative: سَمِيّ can refer to “naming” or “a name” and can also refer to someone with that kind of (special) quality i.e., nobody before has had or shared the same qualities as Yahya (as).
Derivative: From وَصَلَ – يَصِلُ: To be cast into hellfire.
Derivative: أَوْلَىٰ: To have the most priority or be the most deserving.
Derivative: وَارِد: To approach, pass by or to enter.
Derivative: حَتْم is the final verdict that is inevitable with no escape from it. حَتْم means decisively absolute with no doubt.
Derivative: ثُمَّ (then) in contrast to فَ (then) implies التَّراخِي (delay, being after and sometime later)
Derivative: نَدِيٌّ – Means the meeting of high-level decision makers.
Derivative: أَثاث – Refers to furnishings, provisions, resources and utilities.
Derivative: رِءْيًا – In terms of showing off. رِءْيًا is something to look at as a thing of show.
Derivative: That which is returned as a compensation.
Derivative: اِطَّلَعَ means to get hold of news after making some efforts. Literally it means to climb a mountain to discover something.
Derivative: عِتِيّ comes from عُتُوّ referring to someone who has reached the peak in arrogance and rebellion where e is not humbled before anyone.
- This implied meaning is: That my old age has become a bully and rebel that has got a hold of me (عِتِيًّا) and I can’t stand up against him.
Derivative: it is said that: العَزيزُ لَا يُغْلَب (That the ‘Azeez is not defeated or dominated). عِزًّا means indomitable and protective force or might offering strength.
Derivative: ضِدًّا means in opposition. ضِدًّا refers to the enemy who is in opposition to you and the one who tries to make an example of you. It refers to the staunch opponent.
Derivative: أَزَّ – يَؤُزُّ – أَزًّا – To slowly boil meaning to slowly incite where the one whom it is being done to does not even realise it.
Derivative: وَفْدٌ is a group that comes before the king to obtain gifts or grants from him. It implies a delegation that will be honoured.
Derivative: وِرْدًا like مَوْرِد refers to a well that water is drawn from. Here it refers to a place (hell) to quench their thirst.
Derivative: نَسُوقُ is from سَاقَ – يَسُوقُ. It means to herd like animals are herded, to lead and drive them forward.

Derivative: إِدًّا – أَديد is a chaotic sound like a scream or explosion. إِدًّا is something that causes chaos and something at the same time that is extremely offensive and outrageous – a monstrosity and shockingly disgusting.
Derivative: هَيِّنٌ means an extremely easy matter. يَسير means easy but هَيِّن is something that requires no effort and totally effortless.
- “And you were nothing” with the omission of the “نون” from “تَكُن” emphasising the nothingness i.e., you were nothing at all; you didn’t even have the “نون” attached to you in the language.

Derivative: خَرَّ – يَخِرُّ – خُرُور literally means the sound of water dripping and falling. It means to fall down and is also used to portray the meaning of when somebody just collapses and falls quickly or slams to the ground making a thudding noise.
Derivative: اِنْشَقَّ means to crack open in relation to something that is hard.
Derivative: هَدًّا is used when referring to big things falling apart or collapsing.
Derivative: تَفَطَّرَ means to tear apart and is used for things which are long and ripped across.
Derivative: To be appropriate or becoming.
Derivative: the إِن here is إنْ النَّافِيَة (In Naafiyah) which means it the إِن of negation that negates the statement following it.
Derivative: أَحْصَى means to count and keep record. Equivalent to the macro accounting.
Derivative: عَدَّ means to count every single act or statement etc. Equivalent to the micro accounting.

- Ar-Rahman (The Most extremely Merciful) will place a great love (وُدًّا) for them, which means that Allah will have a love for them, they will have a love for Allah and as most of the ‘Ulamaa argue, Allah will place a love in the Muslim for other Muslims.
Derivative: لُّدٌّ is extremely aggressive, argumentative and hateful.
Derivative: الإِحْساس means sense or feel.
Derivative: رِكْز means the slightest possible sound like the buzzing of a fly.
